title: Profile Overview
text: Lovely Yeswanth Panchumarthi is an AI/ML Engineer and Research Scientist based in the US. He specializes in deep learning, high-performance computing infrastructure, and biomedical AI. His work spans foundation models for healthcare data, multimodal RAG pipelines, and production ML systems. He has over 10 peer-reviewed publications and an MS in Computer Science from Emory University.

title: Current Role
text: Currently, Yeswanth is pursuing his MS in Computer Science at Emory University while working as a Graduate Research Assistant. He is also an AI/ML Engineer contributing to open-source AI tooling and research projects. His recent focus is on building practical AI systems — foundation models for ECG/PPG physiological signals, multi-agent LLM pipelines for code review, and efficient local inference infrastructure.

title: Education
text: Yeswanth holds an MS in Computer Science from Emory University. He also worked as a Cloud Computing Assistant in Emory's Office of Information Technology, where he built Python and SLURM tooling to manage HPC cluster workflows.

title: Healthcare AI Research
text: His research centers on biomedical AI, specifically foundation models and multimodal methods for ECG (electrocardiogram) and PPG (photoplethysmography) physiological data. He has published peer-reviewed papers on building and evaluating models that analyze heart signals, with ongoing translational testing in clinical workflows.

title: AI Publications and Contributions
text: Yeswanth has authored or co-authored over 10 peer-reviewed publications in AI and machine learning. His work covers multimodal foundation models, medical image analysis, and efficient inference systems. He actively contributes to open-source AI projects and maintains several repositories on GitHub.

title: Multi-Agent Code Review System
text: A multi-agent LLM code review system that orchestrates multiple specialist reviewers using LangGraph. It combines static code analyzers with LLM-powered syntax, security, and git-history reviewers. The system breaks down a pull request into review tasks, dispatches them to specialist agents, and synthesizes their findings into a coherent report.

title: AeroScript AI
text: A real-time air-gesture drawing platform that uses MediaPipe Hands for hand tracking, TensorFlow.js DoodleNet for sketch recognition, and a multimodal vision-language model for natural-language drawing commands. Users can draw in 3D air space and the system interprets and renders their gestures in real time.

title: ECG Agent
text: A dockerized ECG assistant stack that uses FAISS for embedding-based retrieval, vLLM for fast LLM serving, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools to keep embedding retrieval outside the core LLM loop. It answers medical questions by retrieving relevant ECG findings and synthesizing them with an LLM.

title: Multimodal Document RAG
text: A multimodal RAG pipeline that converts PDF document pages into image embeddings using a vision-language model, then answers user questions by cross-referencing both the text and visual content of the documents. Useful for research papers with charts, diagrams, and mixed-media content.

title: HPC Infrastructure and MLOps
text: Yeswanth has extensive experience building and maintaining HPC and GPU cluster infrastructure. He has set up SLURM-based job schedulers, configured multi-node GPU training environments, and deployed end-to-end ML pipelines on cloud and on-premise clusters.

title: AI and Machine Learning
text: Core AI/ML skills include: Large Language Model (LLM) systems design, RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) pipelines, fine-tuning and evaluation, multimodal model integration, and foundation model research. He works with models ranging from 3B to 400B parameter scales.

title: Programming Languages
text: Primary programming languages are Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, and C++. He also has experience with Java, Go, and SQL for data engineering. Python is his primary language for ML research, data processing, and backend development.

title: ML Frameworks and Tools
text: He uses PyTorch and TensorFlow for deep learning model development, LangChain and LangGraph for LLM orchestration, vLLM and llama.cpp for efficient inference, ChromaDB and FAISS for vector retrieval, and Hugging Face Transformers for model fine-tuning and deployment.

title: Infrastructure and DevOps
text: Infrastructure skills include: Docker and Kubernetes for containerization and orchestration, AWS and GCP cloud platforms, SLURM job scheduling for HPC clusters, GPU cluster management (NVIDIA A100, H100, and Apple Silicon), and CI/CD pipeline design with GitHub Actions and GitLab CI.

title: Web and Full-Stack Development
text: Frontend skills include React, TypeScript, and Vite for building modern web applications. Backend skills include FastAPI and Flask for Python APIs, Node.js for server-side JavaScript, and PostgreSQL/MongoDB for data storage. He builds full-stack AI-powered web applications end-to-end.

title: Portfolio and Contact
text: Portfolio website: https://lyeswanthp.github.io — features sections on Projects, Research, About, Skills, and Contact. GitHub profile: https://github.com/lyeswanthp — contains open-source projects, research code, and AI tooling. For professional inquiries, he can be reached via the contact form on the portfolio site.
